mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 40% of instances when unemployment falls by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable nonfarm payrolls confirms genuine economic recovery rather than temporary inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >55% of cases when both industrial production and payrolls increase simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rebounding above 600 thousand units signals renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts exceed 600 thousand but SPX 60-day returns remain negative or flat in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales directly reflect consumer spending strength, which immediately translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales rise but 5-day SPX returns are negative in >55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in CPI above 0.5 percent month-over-month triggers inflation fears that erode real equity returns, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.5 percent month-over-month but SPX 20-day returns remain positive or flat in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive medium-term S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Both payrolls and GDP grow quarter-over-quarter but SPX 60-day returns are negative in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during liquidity crises signal severe demand destruction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production falls sharply but SPX 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances.
